title: proposal_findings
description: Schema for the LLM-output JSON payload that propose-change and critique consume — an array of proposal findings, each a candidate proposed-change. Distinct from doctor_findings (the doctor static-phase output).

author.description: Optional file-level LLM-self-declared author identifier. Consumed by the unified author-precedence chain (CLI flag > LIVESPEC_AUTHOR_LLM env > this field > 'unknown-llm') in propose-change/critique/revise.

spec_commitments.description: Optional spec→impl commitment declaration per spec.md §'Proposed-change and revision file formats' → 'Spec→impl commitment declaration'. When present, each impl_followups[] entry declares one impl-side work-item that MUST be filed in the active impl-plugin's store before the propose-change is considered closed; supersedes[] (optional) lists earlier id_hint values whose commitments this propose-change absorbs or revokes.

id_hint.description: Author-chosen kebab-case slug pairing the eventual impl-side work-item back to this propose-change via the impl-plugin's spec_commitment_hint field. NOT a generated work-item id.

description.description: One-paragraph description of the impl change required after this propose-change is revised in.

supersedes.description: Optional list of earlier id_hint values whose commitments this propose-change absorbs or revokes. Commitment-coverage tracking is orchestrator-side (the doctor-side unresolved-spec-commitment invariant was retired with the v103 cross-boundary shrink); consumers computing coverage treat the listed id_hints as superseded — no work-item is owed for them.

name.description: Short proposal name; becomes the heading of the resulting `## Proposal: <name>` section in the proposed-change file.

target_spec_files.description: Project-root-relative paths this proposal touches (e.g., "SPECIFICATION/contracts.md"). This field is project-root-relative; do NOT use spec-target-relative paths here.

summary.description: One paragraph stating what changes and why.

motivation.description: The intent input that produced this proposal.

proposed_changes.description: Prose describing the changes, or a unified diff in fenced ```diff blocks, or both.
